mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator simplifies the management of Grafana instances, dashboards, and data sources in Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who prefer GitOps workflows or infrastructure as code. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes CRs and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Grafana Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Grafana Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Grafana Operator.

```bash

helm repo add grafana oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This adds the Grafana Helm charts repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator using Helm, specifying the version to ensure compatibility.

```bash

helm upgrade -i grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

```

> Installs the Grafana Operator.

steps.title: Create a Grafana instance
steps.description:
This step creates a Grafana instance using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

```

> Creates a Grafana instance with specified configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Grafana instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Grafana instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Displays the details of the Grafana instance.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if Grafana instances are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator

kubectl delete grafana grafana

```

> Uninstalls the Grafana Operator.
> Deletes the Grafana instance.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Grafana instances. This step will upgrade the Grafana Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

helm upgrade grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Backs up the current Grafana instance configuration.
> Upgrades the Grafana Operator.
> Verifies the Grafana instance is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating Grafana instance'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the CRD is not installed or the API version is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Grafana CRD is installed correctly and matches the API version.

troubleshooting.title: Grafana instance not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources allocated to the Grafana instance.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in the Grafana CR and adjust as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to access Grafana dashboard
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service type is set to LoadBalancer or NodePort in the Grafana CR.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to data source'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The data source configuration may be incorrect or the data source is down.

**Fix:**
Verify the data source configuration in the Grafana CR and ensure the data source is reachable.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new data source types and improved dashboard management features.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance running with the command 'kubectl get grafana grafana' returning the status as 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
